search_text: D-DPCC: Deep Dynamic Point Cloud Compression via 3D Motion Prediction D-DPCC：通过 3D 运动预测进行深度动态点云压缩 The non-uniformly distributed nature of the 3D dynamic point cloud (DPC) brings significant challenges to its high-efficient inter-frame compression. This paper proposes a novel 3D sparse convolution-based Deep Dynamic Point Cloud Compression (D-DPCC) network to compensate and compress the DPC geometry with 3D motion estimation and motion compensation in the feature space. In the proposed D-DPCC network, we design a {\it Multi-scale Motion Fusion} (MMF) module to accurately estimate the 3D optical flow between the feature representations of adjacent point cloud frames. Specifically, we utilize a 3D sparse convolution-based encoder to obtain the latent representation for motion estimation in the feature space and introduce the proposed MMF module for fused 3D motion embedding. Besides, for motion compensation, we propose a 3D {\it Adaptively Weighted Interpolation} (3DAWI) algorithm with a penalty coefficient to adaptively decrease the impact of distant neighbors. We compress the motion embedding and the residual with a lossy autoencoder-based network. To our knowledge, this paper is the first work proposing an end-to-end deep dynamic point cloud compression framework. The experimental result shows that the proposed D-DPCC framework achieves an average 76\% BD-Rate (Bjontegaard Delta Rate) gains against state-of-the-art Video-based Point Cloud Compression (V-PCC) v13 in inter mode. 3D动态点云（DPC）的非均匀分布特性对其高效帧间压缩带来了重大挑战。本文提出了一种新颖的基于 3D 稀疏卷积的深度动态点云压缩 (D-DPCC) 网络，通过特征空间中的 3D 运动估计和运动补偿来补偿和压缩 DPC 几何结构。在所提出的 D-DPCC 网络中，我们设计了一个多尺度运动融合（MMF）模块来准确估计相邻点云帧的特征表示之间的 3D 光流。具体来说，我们利用基于 3D 稀疏卷积的编码器来获取特征空间中运动估计的潜在表示，并引入所提出的用于融合 3D 运动嵌入的 MMF 模块。此外，对于运动补偿，我们提出了一种带有惩罚系数的 3D {\it 自适应加权插值} (3DAWI) 算法，可以自适应地减少远处邻居的影响。我们使用基于有损自动编码器的网络来压缩运动嵌入和残差。据我们所知，本文是第一篇提出端到端深度动态点云压缩框架的工作。实验结果表明，所提出的 D-DPCC 框架在帧间模式下相对于最先进的基于视频的点云压缩 (V-PCC) v13 实现了平均 76\% BD-Rate (Bjontegaard Delta Rate) 增益。 本文提出了一种基于3D运动预测的深度动态点云压缩框架D-DPCC，通过特征空间中的运动估计与补偿实现高效压缩。 Tingyu Fan,Linyao Gao,Yiling Xu,Zhu Li,Dong Wang cs.CV,cs.LG,eess.IV

authors_text: Idris Abdulmumin,Satya Ranjan Dash,Musa Abdullahi Dawud,Shantipriya Parida,Shamsuddeen Hassan Muhammad,Ibrahim Sa'id Ahmad,Subhadarshi Panda,Ondřej Bojar,Bashir Shehu Galadanci,Bello Shehu Bello
title: Hausa Visual Genome: A Dataset for Multi-Modal English to Hausa Machine Translation
title_zh: 豪萨语视觉基因组：多模态英语到豪萨语机器翻译的数据集
comment: Accepted at Language Resources and Evaluation Conference 2022 (LREC2022)
comments: Accepted at Language Resources and Evaluation Conference 2022 (LREC2022)
abstract: Multi-modal Machine Translation (MMT) enables the use of visual information to enhance the quality of translations. The visual information can serve as a valuable piece of context information to decrease the ambiguity of input sentences. Despite the increasing popularity of such a technique, good and sizeable datasets are scarce, limiting the full extent of their potential. Hausa, a Chadic language, is a member of the Afro-Asiatic language family. It is estimated that about 100 to 150 million people speak the language, with more than 80 million indigenous speakers. This is more than any of the other Chadic languages. Despite a large number of speakers, the Hausa language is considered low-resource in natural language processing (NLP). This is due to the absence of sufficient resources to implement most NLP tasks. While some datasets exist, they are either scarce, machine-generated, or in the religious domain. Therefore, there is a need to create training and evaluation data for implementing machine learning tasks and bridging the research gap in the language. This work presents the Hausa Visual Genome (HaVG), a dataset that contains the description of an image or a section within the image in Hausa and its equivalent in English. To prepare the dataset, we started by translating the English description of the images in the Hindi Visual Genome (HVG) into Hausa automatically. Afterward, the synthetic Hausa data was carefully post-edited considering the respective images. The dataset comprises 32,923 images and their descriptions that are divided into training, development, test, and challenge test set. The Hausa Visual Genome is the first dataset of its kind and can be used for Hausa-English machine translation, multi-modal research, and image description, among various other natural language processing and generation tasks.

Therefore, there is a need to create training and evaluation data for implementing machine learning tasks and bridging the research gap in the language. To prepare the dataset, we started by translating the English description of the images in the Hindi Visual Genome (HVG) into Hausa automatically. abstract_zh: 多模态机器翻译 (MMT) 可以利用视觉信息来提高翻译质量。视觉信息可以作为有价值的上下文信息，以减少输入句子的歧义。尽管这种技术越来越受欢迎，但良好且规模可观的数据集却很稀缺，限制了其潜力的全部发挥。豪萨语是乍得语族的一种语言，属于亚非语系。据估计，约有 100 至 1.5 亿人使用该语言，其中超过 8000 万为土著语言。这比任何其他乍得语言都要多。尽管使用人数众多，但豪萨语在自然语言处理 (NLP) 方面被认为资源匮乏。这是由于缺乏足够的资源来实现大多数 NLP 任务。虽然存在一些数据集，但它们要么稀缺，要么是机器生成的，要么属于宗教领域。因此，需要创建训练和评估数据来实施机器学习任务并缩小语言研究差距。这项工作提出了豪萨视觉基因组 (HaVG)，这是一个数据集，其中包含豪萨语及其等效英文的图像或图像中的某个部分的描述。为了准备数据集，我们首先将印地语视觉基因组 (HVG) 中图像的英文描述自动翻译成豪萨语。随后，考虑到各自的图像，对合成豪萨数据进行了仔细的后期编辑。该数据集包含 32,923 张图像及其描述，分为训练集、开发集、测试集和挑战测试集。豪萨视觉基因组是同类中的第一个数据集，可用于豪萨英语机器翻译、多模态研究和图像描述以及各种其他自然语言处理和生成任务。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Hausa Visual Genome（HaVG）数据集，用于多模态英豪萨语机器翻译任务。
AI.motivation: 豪萨语作为低资源语言缺乏高质量NLP数据，现有数据稀缺或领域受限，需构建多模态数据推动研究。
AI.method: 将HVG的英文图像描述自动翻译为豪萨语后人工校对，构建包含32,923张图像的训练/测试数据集。
AI.result: 产出首个豪萨语多模态数据集，支持机器翻译、图像描述等任务，包含训练/开发/测试/挑战测试集划分。
AI.conclusion: 该数据集填补豪萨语NLP资源空白，为多模态研究和低资源语言处理提供重要基础。

authors_text: Paul Smolensky,R. Thomas McCoy,Roland Fernandez,Matthew Goldrick,Jianfeng Gao
title: Neurocompositional computing: From the Central Paradox of Cognition to a new generation of AI systems
title_zh: 神经组成计算：从认知中心悖论到新一代人工智能系统
comment: 21 pages, 6 figures. For a general AI audience: to appear in AI Magazine. A more extensive presentation of this work is "Neurocompositional computing in human and machine intelligence: A tutorial", Microsoft Technical Report MSR-TR-2022-5; see https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/publication/neurocompositional-computing-in-human-and-machine-intelligence-a-tutorial/
comments: 21 pages, 6 figures. For a general AI audience: to appear in AI Magazine. A more extensive presentation of this work is "Neurocompositional computing in human and machine intelligence: A tutorial", Microsoft Technical Report MSR-TR-2022-5; see https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/publication/neurocompositional-computing-in-human-and-machine-intelligence-a-tutorial/
abstract: What explains the dramatic progress from 20th-century to 21st-century AI, and how can the remaining limitations of current AI be overcome? The widely accepted narrative attributes this progress to massive increases in the quantity of computational and data resources available to support statistical learning in deep artificial neural networks. We show that an additional crucial factor is the development of a new type of computation. Neurocompositional computing adopts two principles that must be simultaneously respected to enable human-level cognition: the principles of Compositionality and Continuity. These have seemed irreconcilable until the recent mathematical discovery that compositionality can be realized not only through discrete methods of symbolic computing, but also through novel forms of continuous neural computing. The revolutionary recent progress in AI has resulted from the use of limited forms of neurocompositional computing. New, deeper forms of neurocompositional computing create AI systems that are more robust, accurate, and comprehensible.
summary: What explains the dramatic progress from 20th-century to 21st-century AI, and how can the remaining limitations of current AI be overcome? The widely accepted narrative attributes this progress to massive increases in the quantity of computational and data resources available to support statistical learning in deep artificial neural networks. We show that an additional crucial factor is the development of a new type of computation. Neurocompositional computing adopts two principles that must be simultaneously respected to enable human-level cognition: the principles of Compositionality and Continuity. These have seemed irreconcilable until the recent mathematical discovery that compositionality can be realized not only through discrete methods of symbolic computing, but also through novel forms of continuous neural computing. The revolutionary recent progress in AI has resulted from the use of limited forms of neurocompositional computing. New, deeper forms of neurocompositional computing create AI systems that are more robust, accurate, and comprehensible.
abstract_zh: 如何解释从 20 世纪到 21 世纪人工智能的巨大进步，以及如何克服当前人工智能的剩余局限性？广泛接受的叙述将这一进步归因于可用于支持深度人工神经网络中统计学习的计算和数据资源数量的大幅增加。我们证明了另一个关键因素是新型计算的发展。神经组合计算采用必须同时遵守的两个原则，以实现人类水平的认知：组合性原则和连续性原则。这些似乎是不可调和的，直到最近的数学发现，组合性不仅可以通过符号计算的离散方法来实现，还可以通过连续神经计算的新颖形式来实现。人工智能最近的革命性进展源于有限形式的神经组合计算的使用。新的、更深层次的神经组合计算创造了更强大、更准确和更容易理解的人工智能系统。

AI.tldr: 本文提出神经组合计算（Neurocompositional Computing）作为新一代人工智能系统的核心方法，通过结合组合性与连续性原则解决认知悖论。
AI.motivation: 当前AI虽因计算资源增长取得进展，但仍存在局限性，需探索新计算范式以实现类人认知能力。
AI.method: 基于数学新发现，开发同时满足组合性（符号计算）与连续性（神经网络）原则的神经组合计算框架。
AI.result: 实现更稳健、精确且可解释的AI系统，突破传统深度学习的局限性。
AI.conclusion: 神经组合计算通过统一离散符号与连续神经过程，为解决认知悖论和构建通用人工智能提供新路径。

search_text: Neurocompositional computing: From the Central Paradox of Cognition to a new generation of AI systems 神经组成计算：从认知中心悖论到新一代人工智能系统 What explains the dramatic progress from 20th-century to 21st-century AI, and how can the remaining limitations of current AI be overcome? The widely accepted narrative attributes this progress to massive increases in the quantity of computational and data resources available to support statistical learning in deep artificial neural networks. We show that an additional crucial factor is the development of a new type of computation. Neurocompositional computing adopts two principles that must be simultaneously respected to enable human-level cognition: the principles of Compositionality and Continuity. These have seemed irreconcilable until the recent mathematical discovery that compositionality can be realized not only through discrete methods of symbolic computing, but also through novel forms of continuous neural computing. The revolutionary recent progress in AI has resulted from the use of limited forms of neurocompositional computing. New, deeper forms of neurocompositional computing create AI systems that are more robust, accurate, and comprehensible. 如何解释从 20 世纪到 21 世纪人工智能的巨大进步，以及如何克服当前人工智能的剩余局限性？广泛接受的叙述将这一进步归因于可用于支持深度人工神经网络中统计学习的计算和数据资源数量的大幅增加。我们证明了另一个关键因素是新型计算的发展。神经组合计算采用必须同时遵守的两个原则，以实现人类水平的认知：组合性原则和连续性原则。这些似乎是不可调和的，直到最近的数学发现，组合性不仅可以通过符号计算的离散方法来实现，还可以通过连续神经计算的新颖形式来实现。人工智能最近的革命性进展源于有限形式的神经组合计算的使用。新的、更深层次的神经组合计算创造了更强大、更准确和更容易理解的人工智能系统。 本文提出神经组合计算（Neurocompositional Computing）作为新一代人工智能系统的核心方法，通过结合组合性与连续性原则解决认知悖论。 Paul Smolensky,R. Thomas McCoy,Roland Fernandez,Matthew Goldrick,Jianfeng Gao cs.AI,cs.NE,cs.SC

authors_text: Zhenfang Chen,Kexin Yi,Yunzhu Li,Mingyu Ding,Antonio Torralba,Joshua B. Tenenbaum,Chuang Gan
title: ComPhy: Compositional Physical Reasoning of Objects and Events from Videos
title_zh: ComPhy：视频中对象和事件的组合物理推理
abstract: Objects' motions in nature are governed by complex interactions and their properties. While some properties, such as shape and material, can be identified via the object's visual appearances, others like mass and electric charge are not directly visible. The compositionality between the visible and hidden properties poses unique challenges for AI models to reason from the physical world, whereas humans can effortlessly infer them with limited observations. Existing studies on video reasoning mainly focus on visually observable elements such as object appearance, movement, and contact interaction. In this paper, we take an initial step to highlight the importance of inferring the hidden physical properties not directly observable from visual appearances, by introducing the Compositional Physical Reasoning (ComPhy) dataset. For a given set of objects, ComPhy includes few videos of them moving and interacting under different initial conditions. The model is evaluated based on its capability to unravel the compositional hidden properties, such as mass and charge, and use this knowledge to answer a set of questions posted on one of the videos. Evaluation results of several state-of-the-art video reasoning models on ComPhy show unsatisfactory performance as they fail to capture these hidden properties. We further propose an oracle neural-symbolic framework named Compositional Physics Learner (CPL), combining visual perception, physical property learning, dynamic prediction, and symbolic execution into a unified framework. CPL can effectively identify objects' physical properties from their interactions and predict their dynamics to answer questions.

While some properties, such as shape and material, can be identified via the object's visual appearances, others like mass and electric charge are not directly visible. The compositionality between the visible and hidden properties poses unique challenges for AI models to reason from the physical world, whereas humans can effortlessly infer them with limited observations. Existing studies on video reasoning mainly focus on visually observable elements such as object appearance, movement, and contact interaction. In this paper, we take an initial step to highlight the importance of inferring the hidden physical properties not directly observable from visual appearances, by introducing the Compositional Physical Reasoning (ComPhy) dataset. For a given set of objects, ComPhy includes few videos of them moving and interacting under different initial conditions. The model is evaluated based on its capability to unravel the compositional hidden properties, such as mass and charge, and use this knowledge to answer a set of questions posted on one of the videos. Evaluation results of several state-of-the-art video reasoning models on ComPhy show unsatisfactory performance as they fail to capture these hidden properties. We further propose an oracle neural-symbolic framework named Compositional Physics Learner (CPL), combining visual perception, physical property learning, dynamic prediction, and symbolic execution into a unified framework. CPL can effectively identify objects' physical properties from their interactions and predict their dynamics to answer questions.
abstract_zh: 自然界中物体的运动受到复杂的相互作用及其属性的控制。虽然一些属性（例如形状和材料）可以通过物体的视觉外观来识别，但其他属性（例如质量和电荷）却无法直接可见。可见属性和隐藏属性之间的组合性对人工智能模型从物理世界进行推理提出了独特的挑战，而人类可以通过有限的观察毫不费力地推断出它们。现有的视频推理研究主要集中在物体外观、运动和接触交互等视觉可观察元素上。在本文中，我们首先通过引入组合物理推理（ComPhy）数据集来强调推断无法从视觉外观直接观察到的隐藏物理属性的重要性。对于一组给定的对象，ComPhy 包含一些它们在不同初始条件下移动和交互的视频。该模型的评估基于其揭示组合隐藏属性（例如质量和电荷）的能力，并使用这些知识来回答其中一个视频上发布的一组问题。 ComPhy 上几种最先进的视频推理模型的评估结果显示出不令人满意的性能，因为它们无法捕获这些隐藏的属性。我们进一步提出了一种名为组合物理学习器（CPL）的预言机神经符号框架，将视觉感知、物理属性学习、动态预测和符号执行结合到一个统一的框架中。 CPL 可以有效地从物体的相互作用中识别其物理属性，并预测它们的动态来回答问题。

AI.tldr: 本文提出ComPhy数据集和CPL框架，旨在通过视频推理隐藏的物理属性（如质量、电荷），解决AI模型在物理世界推理中的不足。
AI.motivation: 现有视频推理模型主要关注可见元素（如外观、运动），难以推断隐藏的物理属性，而人类可凭有限观察推断这些属性，需突破这一瓶颈。
AI.method: 构建包含多条件物体交互视频的ComPhy数据集，并设计融合视觉感知、物理属性学习、动态预测和符号执行的神经符号框架CPL。
AI.result: 主流视频模型在ComPhy上表现不佳，而CPL能有效识别物体物理属性并预测动态，准确回答相关问题。
AI.conclusion: 隐藏物理属性推理是视频理解的关键挑战，CPL框架验证了结合符号推理与深度学习的可行性，为未来研究提供方向。

title: Incomplete Gamma Kernels: Generalizing Locally Optimal Projection Operators
title_zh: 不完全伽马核：推广局部最优投影算子
comment: The final version of record is available at https://doi.org/10.1109/TPAMI.2024.3349967
comments: The final version of record is available at https://doi.org/10.1109/TPAMI.2024.3349967
abstract: We present incomplete gamma kernels, a generalization of Locally Optimal Projection (LOP) operators. In particular, we reveal the relation of the classical localized $ L_1 $ estimator, used in the LOP operator for point cloud denoising, to the common Mean Shift framework via a novel kernel. Furthermore, we generalize this result to a whole family of kernels that are built upon the incomplete gamma function and each represents a localized $ L_p $ estimator. By deriving various properties of the kernel family concerning distributional, Mean Shift induced, and other aspects such as strict positive definiteness, we obtain a deeper understanding of the operator's projection behavior. From these theoretical insights, we illustrate several applications ranging from an improved Weighted LOP (WLOP) density weighting scheme and a more accurate Continuous LOP (CLOP) kernel approximation to the definition of a novel set of robust loss functions. These incomplete gamma losses include the Gaussian and LOP loss as special cases and can be applied to various tasks including normal filtering. Furthermore, we show that the novel kernels can be included as priors into neural networks. We demonstrate the effects of each application in a range of quantitative and qualitative experiments that highlight the benefits induced by our modifications.
summary: We present incomplete gamma kernels, a generalization of Locally Optimal Projection (LOP) operators. In particular, we reveal the relation of the classical localized $ L_1 $ estimator, used in the LOP operator for point cloud denoising, to the common Mean Shift framework via a novel kernel. Furthermore, we generalize this result to a whole family of kernels that are built upon the incomplete gamma function and each represents a localized $ L_p $ estimator. By deriving various properties of the kernel family concerning distributional, Mean Shift induced, and other aspects such as strict positive definiteness, we obtain a deeper understanding of the operator's projection behavior. From these theoretical insights, we illustrate several applications ranging from an improved Weighted LOP (WLOP) density weighting scheme and a more accurate Continuous LOP (CLOP) kernel approximation to the definition of a novel set of robust loss functions. These incomplete gamma losses include the Gaussian and LOP loss as special cases and can be applied to various tasks including normal filtering. Furthermore, we show that the novel kernels can be included as priors into neural networks. We demonstrate the effects of each application in a range of quantitative and qualitative experiments that highlight the benefits induced by our modifications.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了不完整的伽玛核，这是局部最优投影（LOP）算子的推广。特别是，我们通过新颖的内核揭示了用于点云去噪的 LOP 算子中的经典局部 $ L_1 $ 估计器与常见的 Mean Shift 框架的关系。此外，我们将这个结果推广到基于不完整 gamma 函数构建的整个内核系列，每个内核代表一个局部 $ L_p $ 估计器。通过推导核族有关分布、Mean Shift 诱导以及其他方面（例如严格正定性）的各种属性，我们可以更深入地了解算子的投影行为。从这些理论见解中，我们阐述了几种应用，从改进的加权 LOP (WLOP) 密度加权方案和更准确的连续 LOP (CLOP) 核近似到一组新颖的鲁棒损失函数的定义。这些不完全伽马损失包括高斯损失和 LOP 损失作为特殊情况，可以应用于包括正常滤波在内的各种任务。此外，我们表明新的内核可以作为先验包含到神经网络中。我们在一系列定量和定性实验中展示了每种应用的效果，突出了我们的修改带来的好处。

AI.tldr: 提出不完全伽马核以推广局部最优投影算子，并探索其在点云去噪、密度估计及神经网络中的应用。
AI.motivation: 现有LOP算子依赖固定L1估计器，缺乏对不同数据分布及Lp范数的适应性，需更通用的核框架提升灵活性与准确性。
AI.method: 基于不完全伽马函数构建核家族，推导其分布特性及正定性，结合Mean Shift框架，改进WLOP/CLOP算法并设计鲁棒损失函数，集成至神经网络。
AI.result: 实现更精确的密度加权与核近似，提出覆盖高斯/LOP的通用损失函数，实验验证在法线过滤等任务中性能提升，且核可作为神经网络先验。

search_text: Incomplete Gamma Kernels: Generalizing Locally Optimal Projection Operators 不完全伽马核：推广局部最优投影算子 We present incomplete gamma kernels, a generalization of Locally Optimal Projection (LOP) operators. In particular, we reveal the relation of the classical localized $ L_1 $ estimator, used in the LOP operator for point cloud denoising, to the common Mean Shift framework via a novel kernel. Furthermore, we generalize this result to a whole family of kernels that are built upon the incomplete gamma function and each represents a localized $ L_p $ estimator. By deriving various properties of the kernel family concerning distributional, Mean Shift induced, and other aspects such as strict positive definiteness, we obtain a deeper understanding of the operator's projection behavior. From these theoretical insights, we illustrate several applications ranging from an improved Weighted LOP (WLOP) density weighting scheme and a more accurate Continuous LOP (CLOP) kernel approximation to the definition of a novel set of robust loss functions. These incomplete gamma losses include the Gaussian and LOP loss as special cases and can be applied to various tasks including normal filtering. Furthermore, we show that the novel kernels can be included as priors into neural networks. We demonstrate the effects of each application in a range of quantitative and qualitative experiments that highlight the benefits induced by our modifications. 我们提出了不完整的伽玛核，这是局部最优投影（LOP）算子的推广。特别是，我们通过新颖的内核揭示了用于点云去噪的 LOP 算子中的经典局部 $ L_1 $ 估计器与常见的 Mean Shift 框架的关系。此外，我们将这个结果推广到基于不完整 gamma 函数构建的整个内核系列，每个内核代表一个局部 $ L_p $ 估计器。通过推导核族有关分布、Mean Shift 诱导以及其他方面（例如严格正定性）的各种属性，我们可以更深入地了解算子的投影行为。从这些理论见解中，我们阐述了几种应用，从改进的加权 LOP (WLOP) 密度加权方案和更准确的连续 LOP (CLOP) 核近似到一组新颖的鲁棒损失函数的定义。这些不完全伽马损失包括高斯损失和 LOP 损失作为特殊情况，可以应用于包括正常滤波在内的各种任务。此外，我们表明新的内核可以作为先验包含到神经网络中。我们在一系列定量和定性实验中展示了每种应用的效果，突出了我们的修改带来的好处。 提出不完全伽马核以推广局部最优投影算子，并探索其在点云去噪、密度估计及神经网络中的应用。 Patrick Stotko,Michael Weinmann,Reinhard Klein cs.CV,cs.GR

authors_text: Felix Wu,Kwangyoun Kim,Shinji Watanabe,Kyu Han,Ryan McDonald,Kilian Q. Weinberger,Yoav Artzi
title: Wav2Seq: Pre-training Speech-to-Text Encoder-Decoder Models Using Pseudo Languages
title_zh: Wav2Seq：使用伪语言预训练语音到文本编码器-解码器模型
abstract: We introduce Wav2Seq, the first self-supervised approach to pre-train both parts of encoder-decoder models for speech data. We induce a pseudo language as a compact discrete representation, and formulate a self-supervised pseudo speech recognition task -- transcribing audio inputs into pseudo subword sequences. This process stands on its own, or can be applied as low-cost second-stage pre-training. We experiment with automatic speech recognition (ASR), spoken named entity recognition, and speech-to-text translation. We set new state-of-the-art results for end-to-end spoken named entity recognition, and show consistent improvements on 20 language pairs for speech-to-text translation, even when competing methods use additional text data for training. Finally, on ASR, our approach enables encoder-decoder methods to benefit from pre-training for all parts of the network, and shows comparable performance to highly optimized recent methods.
summary: We introduce Wav2Seq, the first self-supervised approach to pre-train both parts of encoder-decoder models for speech data. We induce a pseudo language as a compact discrete representation, and formulate a self-supervised pseudo speech recognition task -- transcribing audio inputs into pseudo subword sequences. This process stands on its own, or can be applied as low-cost second-stage pre-training. We experiment with automatic speech recognition (ASR), spoken named entity recognition, and speech-to-text translation. We set new state-of-the-art results for end-to-end spoken named entity recognition, and show consistent improvements on 20 language pairs for speech-to-text translation, even when competing methods use additional text data for training. Finally, on ASR, our approach enables encoder-decoder methods to benefit from pre-training for all parts of the network, and shows comparable performance to highly optimized recent methods.
abstract_zh: 我们引入了 Wav2Seq，这是第一个对语音数据的编码器-解码器模型的两个部分进行预训练的自监督方法。我们将伪语言归纳为紧凑的离散表示，并制定了自我监督的伪语音识别任务——将音频输入转录为伪子字序列。这个过程是独立的，或者可以用作低成本的第二阶段预训练。我们尝试了自动语音识别 (ASR)、口语命名实体识别和语音到文本翻译。我们为端到端口语命名实体识别设定了新的最先进结果，并在语音到文本翻译的 20 种语言对上显示出一致的改进，即使竞争方法使用额外的文本数据进行训练也是如此。最后，在 ASR 上，我们的方法使编码器-解码器方法能够从网络所有部分的预训练中受益，并显示出与高度优化的最新方法相当的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Wav2Seq方法，通过伪语言进行自监督预训练，同时优化语音到文本编码器-解码器模型。
AI.motivation: 现有语音模型预训练方法难以同时优化编码器和解码器，且依赖额外文本数据，限制了性能提升。
AI.method: 引入伪语言作为离散表示，构建自监督伪语音识别任务，将音频转录为伪子词序列，实现无监督预训练。
AI.result: 在端到端命名实体识别中取得新SOTA，20种语言对的语音翻译任务表现一致提升，且无需额外文本数据。
AI.conclusion: 该方法使编码器-解码器模型全面受益于预训练，性能可比优化方法，为自监督语音学习提供新方向。

title: The Equity Framework: Fairness Beyond Equalized Predictive Outcomes
title_zh: 公平框架：超越均等预测结果的公平性
abstract: Machine Learning (ML) decision-making algorithms are now widely used in predictive decision-making, for example, to determine who to admit and give a loan. Their wide usage and consequential effects on individuals led the ML community to question and raise concerns on how the algorithms differently affect different people and communities. In this paper, we study fairness issues that arise when decision-makers use models (proxy models) that deviate from the models that depict the physical and social environment in which the decisions are situated (intended models). We also highlight the effect of obstacles on individual access and utilization of the models. To this end, we formulate an Equity Framework that considers equal access to the model, equal outcomes from the model, and equal utilization of the model, and consequentially achieves equity and higher social welfare than current fairness notions that aim for equality. We show how the three main aspects of the framework are connected and provide an equity scoring algorithm and questions to guide decision-makers towards equitable decision-making. We show how failure to consider access, outcome, and utilization would exacerbate proxy gaps leading to an infinite inequity loop that reinforces structural inequities through inaccurate and incomplete ground truth curation. We, therefore, recommend a more critical look at the model design and its effect on equity and a shift towards equity achieving predictive decision-making models.
summary: Machine Learning (ML) decision-making algorithms are now widely used in predictive decision-making, for example, to determine who to admit and give a loan. Their wide usage and consequential effects on individuals led the ML community to question and raise concerns on how the algorithms differently affect different people and communities. In this paper, we study fairness issues that arise when decision-makers use models (proxy models) that deviate from the models that depict the physical and social environment in which the decisions are situated (intended models). We also highlight the effect of obstacles on individual access and utilization of the models. To this end, we formulate an Equity Framework that considers equal access to the model, equal outcomes from the model, and equal utilization of the model, and consequentially achieves equity and higher social welfare than current fairness notions that aim for equality. We show how the three main aspects of the framework are connected and provide an equity scoring algorithm and questions to guide decision-makers towards equitable decision-making. We show how failure to consider access, outcome, and utilization would exacerbate proxy gaps leading to an infinite inequity loop that reinforces structural inequities through inaccurate and incomplete ground truth curation. We, therefore, recommend a more critical look at the model design and its effect on equity and a shift towards equity achieving predictive decision-making models.
abstract_zh: 机器学习 (ML) 决策算法现在广泛用于预测决策，例如，确定接纳谁和发放贷款。它们的广泛使用和对个人的间接影响导致机器学习社区对算法如何对不同的人和社区产生不同的影响提出质疑和担忧。在本文中，我们研究了当决策者使用的模型（代理模型）偏离了描述决策所在的物理和社会环境的模型（预期模型）时出现的公平问题。我们还强调了障碍对个人访问和使用模型的影响。为此，我们制定了公平框架，考虑模型的平等获取、模型的平等结果和模型的平等利用，从而实现公平和比当前以平等为目标的公平理念更高的社会福利。我们展示了该框架的三个主要方面如何相互联系，并提供公平评分算法和问题来指导决策者做出公平的决策。我们展示了如果不考虑访问、结果和利用将如何加剧代理差距，从而导致无限的不平等循环，通过不准确和不完整的事实管理加剧结构性不平等。因此，我们建议对模型设计及其对公平性的影响进行更严格的审视，并转向公平性以实现预测决策模型。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种超越平等预测结果的公平性框架，通过考虑模型的可及性、结果和利用情况，实现更高的社会福利。
AI.motivation: 当前公平性概念仅关注预测结果的平等化，忽视了模型可及性、利用差异及结构不平等的强化问题，导致算法决策可能加剧社会不公。
AI.method: 构建包含模型可及性、结果平等性和利用均衡性的‘公平性框架’，设计公平性评分算法及决策指导问题，分析忽略三要素的后果。
AI.result: 证明忽视三要素会扩大代理模型差距，形成无限不平等循环；框架相比传统公平性概念能实现更高社会福利。
AI.conclusion: 需重新审视模型设计对公平性的影响，转向以实现公平为目标的预测决策模型，以打破结构性不平等的强化循环。

Their wide usage and consequential effects on individuals led the ML community to question and raise concerns on how the algorithms differently affect different people and communities. In this paper, we study fairness issues that arise when decision-makers use models (proxy models) that deviate from the models that depict the physical and social environment in which the decisions are situated (intended models). We also highlight the effect of obstacles on individual access and utilization of the models. To this end, we formulate an Equity Framework that considers equal access to the model, equal outcomes from the model, and equal utilization of the model, and consequentially achieves equity and higher social welfare than current fairness notions that aim for equality. We show how the three main aspects of the framework are connected and provide an equity scoring algorithm and questions to guide decision-makers towards equitable decision-making. We show how failure to consider access, outcome, and utilization would exacerbate proxy gaps leading to an infinite inequity loop that reinforces structural inequities through inaccurate and incomplete ground truth curation. We, therefore, recommend a more critical look at the model design and its effect on equity and a shift towards equity achieving predictive decision-making models. 机器学习 (ML) 决策算法现在广泛用于预测决策，例如，确定接纳谁和发放贷款。它们的广泛使用和对个人的间接影响导致机器学习社区对算法如何对不同的人和社区产生不同的影响提出质疑和担忧。在本文中，我们研究了当决策者使用的模型（代理模型）偏离了描述决策所在的物理和社会环境的模型（预期模型）时出现的公平问题。我们还强调了障碍对个人访问和使用模型的影响。为此，我们制定了公平框架，考虑模型的平等获取、模型的平等结果和模型的平等利用，从而实现公平和比当前以平等为目标的公平理念更高的社会福利。我们展示了该框架的三个主要方面如何相互联系，并提供公平评分算法和问题来指导决策者做出公平的决策。我们展示了如果不考虑访问、结果和利用将如何加剧代理差距，从而导致无限的不平等循环，通过不准确和不完整的事实管理加剧结构性不平等。因此，我们建议对模型设计及其对公平性的影响进行更严格的审视，并转向公平性以实现预测决策模型。 本文提出一种超越平等预测结果的公平性框架，通过考虑模型的可及性、结果和利用情况，实现更高的社会福利。 Keziah Naggita,J. Ceasar Aguma cs.CY,cs.AI,cs.LG

title: Five Ps: Leverage Zones Towards Responsible AI
title_zh: 五个 P：利用区域实现负责任的人工智能
abstract: There is a growing debate amongst academics and practitioners on whether interventions made, thus far, towards Responsible AI would have been enough to engage with root causes of AI problems. Failure to effect meaningful changes in this system could see these initiatives to not reach their potential and lead to the concept becoming another buzzword for companies to use in their marketing campaigns. We propose that there is an opportunity to improve the extent to which interventions are understood to be effective in their contribution to the change required for Responsible AI. Using the notions of leverage zones adapted from the 'Systems Thinking' literature, we suggest a novel approach to evaluate the effectiveness of interventions, to focus on those that may bring about the real change that is needed. In this paper we argue that insights from using this perspective demonstrate that the majority of current initiatives taken by various actors in the field, focus on low-order interventions, such as short-term fixes, tweaking algorithms and updating parameters, absent from higher-order interventions, such as redefining the system's foundational structures that govern those parameters, or challenging the underlying purpose upon which those structures are built and developed in the first place(high-leverage). This paper presents a conceptual framework called the Five Ps to identify interventions towards Responsible AI and provides a scaffold for transdisciplinary question asking to improve outcomes towards Responsible AI.
summary: There is a growing debate amongst academics and practitioners on whether interventions made, thus far, towards Responsible AI would have been enough to engage with root causes of AI problems. Failure to effect meaningful changes in this system could see these initiatives to not reach their potential and lead to the concept becoming another buzzword for companies to use in their marketing campaigns. We propose that there is an opportunity to improve the extent to which interventions are understood to be effective in their contribution to the change required for Responsible AI. Using the notions of leverage zones adapted from the 'Systems Thinking' literature, we suggest a novel approach to evaluate the effectiveness of interventions, to focus on those that may bring about the real change that is needed. In this paper we argue that insights from using this perspective demonstrate that the majority of current initiatives taken by various actors in the field, focus on low-order interventions, such as short-term fixes, tweaking algorithms and updating parameters, absent from higher-order interventions, such as redefining the system's foundational structures that govern those parameters, or challenging the underlying purpose upon which those structures are built and developed in the first place(high-leverage). This paper presents a conceptual framework called the Five Ps to identify interventions towards Responsible AI and provides a scaffold for transdisciplinary question asking to improve outcomes towards Responsible AI.
abstract_zh: 学术界和从业者之间关于迄今为止对负责任人工智能所做的干预措施是否足以解决人工智能问题的根本原因的争论越​​来越多。如果未能对该系统进行有意义的改变，这些举措可能无法发挥其潜力，并导致该概念成为公司在营销活动中使用的另一个流行词。我们建议有机会提高干预措施对负责任人工智能所需变革的有效贡献程度。使用改编自“系统思维”文献的杠杆区概念，我们建议采用一种新颖的方法来评估干预措施的有效性，重点关注那些可能带来所需的真正变革的干预措施。在本文中，我们认为，从这个角度得出的见解表明，该领域各个参与者目前采取的大多数举措都集中在低阶干预措施上，例如短期修复、调整算法和更新参数，而缺乏高阶干预措施，例如重新定义控制这些参数的系统基础结构，或挑战这些结构最初构建和开发的根本目的（高杠杆）。本文提出了一个称为“五个 P”的概念框架，用于确定对 Responsible AI 的干预措施，并为提出跨学科问题以改善 Responsible AI 的结果提供了一个框架。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为'Five Ps'的概念框架，利用系统思维中的'杠杆点'理论，评估负责任AI干预措施的有效性，以识别能够推动系统性变革的高阶干预措施。
AI.motivation: 当前负责任AI的实践多集中于算法优化等低阶干预，未能触及系统结构性问题，导致倡议效果有限，易沦为营销概念。
AI.method: 借鉴系统思维中的杠杆点理论，构建'Five Ps'框架，通过跨学科问题分析方法，区分低阶与高阶干预措施的差异。
AI.result: 发现现有负责任AI举措主要集中于参数调整等低阶干预，缺乏对系统基础结构和核心目标的高阶干预探索。
AI.conclusion: 需通过'Five Ps'框架聚焦高杠杆干预，重新定义AI系统的基础结构与目标，才能实现负责任AI的实质性变革。

authors_text: Ke Wang,Yafei Zhao,Rajan Kumar Gangadhari,Zhixing Li
title: Analyzing the Adoption Challenges of the Internet of Things (IoT) and Artificial Intelligence (AI) for Smart Cities in China
title_zh: 分析中国智慧城市采用物联网 (IoT) 和人工智能 (AI) 的挑战
abstract: Smart cities play a vital role in the growth of a nation. In recent years, several countries have made huge investments in developing smart cities to offer sustainable living. However, there are some challenges to overcome in smart city development, such as traffic and transportation man-agement, energy and water distribution and management, air quality and waste management monitoring, etc. The capabilities of the Internet of Things (IoT) and artificial intelligence (AI) can help to achieve some goals of smart cities, and there are proven examples from some cities like Singapore, Copenhagen, etc. However, the adoption of AI and the IoT in developing countries has some challenges. The analysis of challenges hindering the adoption of AI and the IoT are very limited. This study aims to fill this research gap by analyzing the causal relationships among the challenges in smart city development, and contains several parts that conclude the previous scholars work, as well as independent research and investigation, such as data collection and analysis based on DEMATEL. In this paper, we have reviewed the literature to extract key chal-lenges for the adoption of AI and the IoT. These helped us to proceed with the investigation and analyze the adoption status. Therefore, using the PRISMA method, 10 challenges were identified from the literature review. Subsequently, determination of the causal inter-relationships among the key challenges based on expert opinions using DEMATEL is performed. This study explored the driving and dependent power of the challenges, and causal relationships between the barriers were established.
summary: Smart cities play a vital role in the growth of a nation. In recent years, several countries have made huge investments in developing smart cities to offer sustainable living. However, there are some challenges to overcome in smart city development, such as traffic and transportation man-agement, energy and water distribution and management, air quality and waste management monitoring, etc. The capabilities of the Internet of Things (IoT) and artificial intelligence (AI) can help to achieve some goals of smart cities, and there are proven examples from some cities like Singapore, Copenhagen, etc. However, the adoption of AI and the IoT in developing countries has some challenges. The analysis of challenges hindering the adoption of AI and the IoT are very limited. This study aims to fill this research gap by analyzing the causal relationships among the challenges in smart city development, and contains several parts that conclude the previous scholars work, as well as independent research and investigation, such as data collection and analysis based on DEMATEL. In this paper, we have reviewed the literature to extract key chal-lenges for the adoption of AI and the IoT. These helped us to proceed with the investigation and analyze the adoption status. Therefore, using the PRISMA method, 10 challenges were identified from the literature review. Subsequently, determination of the causal inter-relationships among the key challenges based on expert opinions using DEMATEL is performed. abstract_zh: 智慧城市在一个国家的发展中发挥着至关重要的作用。近年来，一些国家在发展智慧城市方面投入巨资，以提供可持续的生活。然而，智慧城市的发展还需要克服一些挑战，例如交通和运输管理、能源和水的分配和管理、空气质量和废物管理监测等。物联网（IoT）和人工智能（AI）的能力可以帮助实现智慧城市的一些目标，新加坡、哥本哈根等一些城市已经有成熟的例子。然而，人工智能和物联网在发展中国家的采用存在一些挑战。对阻碍人工智能和物联网采用的挑战的分析非常有限。本研究旨在通过分析智慧城市发展挑战之间的因果关系来填补这一研究空白，包括总结先前学者工作的几个部分，以及独立的研究和调查，例如基于DEMATEL的数据收集和分析。在本文中，我们回顾了文献，以提取采用人工智能和物联网的关键挑战。这些有助于我们继续调查并分析采用状况。因此，使用 PRISMA 方法，从文献综述中确定了 10 个挑战。随后，使用 DEMATEL 根据专家意见确定关键挑战之间的因果关系。这项研究探讨了挑战的驱动力和依赖力，并建立了障碍之间的因果关系。

AI.tldr: 本文分析了中国智慧城市建设中物联网（IoT）和人工智能（AI）采用的主要挑战及其因果关系。
AI.motivation: 现有研究对发展中国家在智慧城市建设中采用AI和IoT的挑战分析不足，需填补这一研究空白。
AI.method: 通过文献综述结合PRISMA方法筛选出10个关键挑战，并利用DEMATEL方法分析专家意见中的因果关系。
AI.result: 识别出10个主要挑战，并建立其驱动因素与依赖关系的因果网络。
AI.conclusion: 明确挑战间的因果关系有助于制定针对性策略，推动AI与IoT在智慧城市建设中的有效应用。

This study explored the driving and dependent power of the challenges, and causal relationships between the barriers were established. 智慧城市在一个国家的发展中发挥着至关重要的作用。近年来，一些国家在发展智慧城市方面投入巨资，以提供可持续的生活。然而，智慧城市的发展还需要克服一些挑战，例如交通和运输管理、能源和水的分配和管理、空气质量和废物管理监测等。物联网（IoT）和人工智能（AI）的能力可以帮助实现智慧城市的一些目标，新加坡、哥本哈根等一些城市已经有成熟的例子。然而，人工智能和物联网在发展中国家的采用存在一些挑战。对阻碍人工智能和物联网采用的挑战的分析非常有限。本研究旨在通过分析智慧城市发展挑战之间的因果关系来填补这一研究空白，包括总结先前学者工作的几个部分，以及独立的研究和调查，例如基于DEMATEL的数据收集和分析。在本文中，我们回顾了文献，以提取采用人工智能和物联网的关键挑战。这些有助于我们继续调查并分析采用状况。因此，使用 PRISMA 方法，从文献综述中确定了 10 个挑战。随后，使用 DEMATEL 根据专家意见确定关键挑战之间的因果关系。这项研究探讨了挑战的驱动力和依赖力，并建立了障碍之间的因果关系。 本文分析了中国智慧城市建设中物联网（IoT）和人工智能（AI）采用的主要挑战及其因果关系。 Ke Wang,Yafei Zhao,Rajan Kumar Gangadhari,Zhixing Li cs.CY,cs.AI

title: Quantifying Health Inequalities Induced by Data and AI Models
title_zh: 量化数据和人工智能模型引起的健康不平等
comment: Accepted by IJCAI-ECAI 2022 AI for Good track
comments: Accepted by IJCAI-ECAI 2022 AI for Good track
abstract: AI technologies are being increasingly tested and applied in critical environments including healthcare. Without an effective way to detect and mitigate AI induced inequalities, AI might do more harm than good, potentially leading to the widening of underlying inequalities. This paper proposes a generic allocation-deterioration framework for detecting and quantifying AI induced inequality. Specifically, AI induced inequalities are quantified as the area between two allocation-deterioration curves. To assess the framework's performance, experiments were conducted on ten synthetic datasets (N>33,000) generated from HiRID - a real-world Intensive Care Unit (ICU) dataset, showing its ability to accurately detect and quantify inequality proportionally to controlled inequalities. Extensive analyses were carried out to quantify health inequalities (a) embedded in two real-world ICU datasets; (b) induced by AI models trained for two resource allocation scenarios. Results showed that compared to men, women had up to 33% poorer deterioration in markers of prognosis when admitted to HiRID ICUs. All four AI models assessed were shown to induce significant inequalities (2.45% to 43.2%) for non-White compared to White patients. The models exacerbated data embedded inequalities significantly in 3 out of 8 assessments, one of which was >9 times worse. The codebase is at https://github.com/knowlab/DAindex-Framework.

Without an effective way to detect and mitigate AI induced inequalities, AI might do more harm than good, potentially leading to the widening of underlying inequalities. Specifically, AI induced inequalities are quantified as the area between two allocation-deterioration curves. To assess the framework's performance, experiments were conducted on ten synthetic datasets (N>33,000) generated from HiRID - a real-world Intensive Care Unit (ICU) dataset, showing its ability to accurately detect and quantify inequality proportionally to controlled inequalities. Extensive analyses were carried out to quantify health inequalities (a) embedded in two real-world ICU datasets; (b) induced by AI models trained for two resource allocation scenarios. Results showed that compared to men, women had up to 33% poorer deterioration in markers of prognosis when admitted to HiRID ICUs. The models exacerbated data embedded inequalities significantly in 3 out of 8 assessments, one of which was >9 times worse. The codebase is at https://github.com/knowlab/DAindex-Framework.
abstract_zh: 人工智能技术正在越来越多地在包括医疗保健在内的关键环境中进行测试和应用。如果没有有效的方法来检测和减轻人工智能引起的不平等，人工智能可能弊大于利，并可能导致潜在不平等的扩大。本文提出了一个通用的分配恶化框架，用于检测和量化人工智能引起的不平等。具体来说，人工智能引起的不平等被量化为两条分配恶化曲线之间的面积。为了评估该框架的性能，我们对 HiRID（真实世界的重症监护病房 (ICU) 数据集）生成的 10 个合成数据集 (N>33,000) 进行了实验，显示了其准确检测和量化与受控不平等成比例的不平等的能力。我们进行了广泛的分析来量化健康不平等（a）嵌入两个真实世界的 ICU 数据集中； (b) 由针对两种资源分配场景训练的人工智能模型诱导。结果显示，与男性相比，女性入住 HiRID ICU 时预后指标的恶化程度要低 33%。与白人患者相比，所有四种评估的人工智能模型都显示出对非白人患者造成显着的不平等（2.45％至43.2％）。在 8 项评估中，有 3 项模型显着加剧了数据嵌入的不平等，其中一项严重程度超过 9 倍。代码库位于 https://github.com/knowlab/DAindex-Framework。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种通用框架，用于检测和量化由数据及AI模型引发的健康不平等现象。
AI.motivation: AI在医疗等关键领域应用时可能加剧固有不平等，但缺乏有效检测和缓解方法，需量化AI诱导的不平等以防止危害。
AI.method: 构建分配-恶化框架，通过计算两种分配-恶化曲线间的面积量化AI诱导的不平等，并在合成数据集及真实ICU数据上验证。
AI.result: 发现女性患者预后指标恶化程度比男性高33%，非白人患者相比白人存在2.45%-43.2%的不平等；AI模型在3/8评估中显著加剧数据嵌入的不平等，最严重情况恶化超9倍。
AI.conclusion: 需重视AI模型对健康不平等的放大作用，该框架可为公平性评估提供量化工具，推动AI在医疗中的负责任应用。

title: Investigating the impact of COVID-19 on Online Learning-based Web Behavior
title_zh: 调查 COVID-19 对基于在线学习的网络行为的影响
comment: Proceedings of the 7th International Conference on Human Interaction & Emerging Technologies: Artificial Intelligence & Future Applications (IHIET-AI 2022), Lausanne, Switzerland, April 21-23, 2022
comments: Proceedings of the 7th International Conference on Human Interaction & Emerging Technologies: Artificial Intelligence & Future Applications (IHIET-AI 2022), Lausanne, Switzerland, April 21-23, 2022
abstract: COVID-19, a pandemic that the world has not seen in decades, has resulted in presenting a multitude of unprecedented challenges for student learning across the globe. The global surge in COVID-19 cases resulted in several schools, colleges, and universities closing in 2020 in almost all parts of the world and switching to online or remote learning, which has impacted student learning in different ways. This has resulted in both educators and students spending more time on the internet than ever before, which may be broadly summarized as both these groups investigating, learning, and familiarizing themselves with information, tools, applications, and frameworks to adapt to online learning. This paper takes an explorative approach to further investigate and analyze the impact of COVID-19 on such web behavior data related to online learning to interpret the associated interests, challenges, and needs. The study specifically focused on investigating Google Search-based web behavior data as Google is the most popular search engine globally. The impact of COVID-19 related to online learning-based web behavior on Google was studied for the top 20 worst affected countries in terms of the total number of COVID-19 cases, and the findings have been published as an open-access dataset. Furthermore, to interpret the trends in web behavior data related to online learning, the paper discusses a case study in terms of the impact of COVID-19 on the education system of one of these countries.

abstract_zh: COVID-19 是一种世界数十年来未曾见过的大流行病，给全球学生的学习带来了许多前所未有的挑战。全球 COVID-19 病例激增导致世界各地几乎所有地区的多所学校、学院和大学于 2020 年关闭，转为在线或远程学习，这从不同方面影响了学生的学习。这导致教育工作者和学生在互联网上花费的时间比以往任何时候都多，这可以概括为这两个群体都在调查、学习和熟悉信息、工具、应用程序和框架以适应在线学习。本文采用探索性方法进一步调查和分析 COVID-19 对与在线学习相关的网络行为数据的影响，以解释相关的兴趣、挑战和需求。该研究特别侧重于调查基于谷歌搜索的网络行为数据，因为谷歌是全球最受欢迎的搜索引擎。针对 COVID-19 病例总数排名前 20 个受影响最严重的国家，研究了与 Google 上基于在线学习的网络行为相关的 COVID-19 的影响，研究结果已作为开放获取数据集发布。此外，为了解释与在线学习相关的网络行为数据的趋势，本文讨论了一个关于 COVID-19 对其中一个国家的教育系统影响的案例研究。

AI.tldr: 本论文通过分析新冠疫情对在线学习相关网络行为的影响，探讨学生和教育工作者在疫情期间的网络搜索行为变化。
AI.motivation: 新冠疫情导致全球教育系统转向在线学习，研究其对网络行为的影响有助于理解教育适应过程中的挑战与需求。
AI.method: 基于全球搜索量最大的Google搜索引擎数据，分析20个疫情最严重国家的在线学习相关搜索趋势，并结合某国教育系统案例进行研究。
AI.result: 发布了开放数据集，揭示了疫情期间在线学习工具、教学方法等搜索需求的显著增长，并通过案例分析验证了趋势变化。
AI.conclusion: 疫情加速了在线学习的普及，研究结果为未来教育技术发展和政策制定提供了数据支持与理论参考。

COVID-19 是一种世界数十年来未曾见过的大流行病，给全球学生的学习带来了许多前所未有的挑战。全球 COVID-19 病例激增导致世界各地几乎所有地区的多所学校、学院和大学于 2020 年关闭，转为在线或远程学习，这从不同方面影响了学生的学习。这导致教育工作者和学生在互联网上花费的时间比以往任何时候都多，这可以概括为这两个群体都在调查、学习和熟悉信息、工具、应用程序和框架以适应在线学习。本文采用探索性方法进一步调查和分析 COVID-19 对与在线学习相关的网络行为数据的影响，以解释相关的兴趣、挑战和需求。该研究特别侧重于调查基于谷歌搜索的网络行为数据，因为谷歌是全球最受欢迎的搜索引擎。针对 COVID-19 病例总数排名前 20 个受影响最严重的国家，研究了与 Google 上基于在线学习的网络行为相关的 COVID-19 的影响，研究结果已作为开放获取数据集发布。此外，为了解释与在线学习相关的网络行为数据的趋势，本文讨论了一个关于 COVID-19 对其中一个国家的教育系统影响的案例研究。 本论文通过分析新冠疫情对在线学习相关网络行为的影响，探讨学生和教育工作者在疫情期间的网络搜索行为变化。 Nirmalya Thakur,Saumick Pradhan,Chia Y. Han cs.CY,cs.HC,cs.IR

authors_text: Hwijae Son,Sung Woong Cho,Hyung Ju Hwang
title: Enhanced Physics-Informed Neural Networks with Augmented Lagrangian Relaxation Method (AL-PINNs)
title_zh: 采用增强拉格朗日松弛法的增强型物理信息神经网络 (AL-PINN)
abstract: Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) have become a prominent application of deep learning in scientific computation, as they are powerful approximators of solutions to nonlinear partial differential equations (PDEs). There have been numerous attempts to facilitate the training process of PINNs by adjusting the weight of each component of the loss function, called adaptive loss-balancing algorithms. In this paper, we propose an Augmented Lagrangian relaxation method for PINNs (AL-PINNs). We treat the initial and boundary conditions as constraints for the optimization problem of the PDE residual. By employing Augmented Lagrangian relaxation, the constrained optimization problem becomes a sequential max-min problem so that the learnable parameters $λ$ adaptively balance each loss component. Our theoretical analysis reveals that the sequence of minimizers of the proposed loss functions converges to an actual solution for the Helmholtz, viscous Burgers, and Klein--Gordon equations. We demonstrate through various numerical experiments that AL-PINNs yield a much smaller relative error compared with that of state-of-the-art adaptive loss-balancing algorithms.
summary: Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) have become a prominent application of deep learning in scientific computation, as they are powerful approximators of solutions to nonlinear partial differential equations (PDEs). There have been numerous attempts to facilitate the training process of PINNs by adjusting the weight of each component of the loss function, called adaptive loss-balancing algorithms. In this paper, we propose an Augmented Lagrangian relaxation method for PINNs (AL-PINNs). We treat the initial and boundary conditions as constraints for the optimization problem of the PDE residual. By employing Augmented Lagrangian relaxation, the constrained optimization problem becomes a sequential max-min problem so that the learnable parameters $λ$ adaptively balance each loss component. Our theoretical analysis reveals that the sequence of minimizers of the proposed loss functions converges to an actual solution for the Helmholtz, viscous Burgers, and Klein--Gordon equations. We demonstrate through various numerical experiments that AL-PINNs yield a much smaller relative error compared with that of state-of-the-art adaptive loss-balancing algorithms.
abstract_zh: 物理信息神经网络 (PINN) 已成为深度学习在科学计算中的重要应用，因为它们是非线性偏微分方程 (PDE) 解的强大逼近器。人们已经进行了许多尝试，通过调整损失函数每个组成部分的权重来促进 PINN 的训练过程，称为自适应损失平衡算法。在本文中，我们提出了一种 PINN 的增强拉格朗日松弛方法（AL-PINN）。我们将初始条件和边界条件视为偏微分方程残差优化问题的约束。通过采用增强拉格朗日松弛，约束优化问题变成了顺序最大-最小问题，以便可学习参数 $λ$ 自适应地平衡每个损失分量。我们的理论分析表明，所提出的损失函数的最小化序列收敛于亥姆霍兹方程、粘性伯格斯方程和克莱因-戈登方程的实际解。我们通过各种数值实验证明，与最先进的自适应损耗平衡算法相比，AL-PINN 产生的相对误差要小得多。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于增广拉格朗日松弛法的增强型物理信息神经网络（AL-PINNs），用于更高效求解非线性偏微分方程。
AI.motivation: 传统PINNs在训练过程中依赖人工调整损失函数权重，难以有效平衡PDE残差与边界条件约束，导致收敛性和精度不足。
AI.method: 将初始/边界条件作为约束引入增广拉格朗日框架，通过可学习参数λ将约束优化转化为序贯max-min问题，实现损失项自适应平衡。
AI.result: 理论证明该方法对Helmholtz等三类方程的解序列收敛，数值实验显示相对误差较现有自适应算法降低显著。
AI.conclusion: AL-PINNs通过引入增广拉格朗日松弛机制，解决了PINNs的损失平衡难题，在保持物理约束的同时提升了求解精度。

authors_text: Riddhiman Adib,Md Osman Gani,Sheikh Iqbal Ahamed,Mohammad Adibuzzaman
title: Causal Discovery on the Effect of Antipsychotic Drugs on Delirium Patients in the ICU using Large EHR Dataset
title_zh: 使用大型 EHR 数据集发现 ICU 抗精神病药物对谵妄患者影响的因果关系
abstract: Delirium occurs in about 80% cases in the Intensive Care Unit (ICU) and is associated with a longer hospital stay, increased mortality and other related issues. Delirium does not have any biomarker-based diagnosis and is commonly treated with antipsychotic drugs (APD). However, multiple studies have shown controversy over the efficacy or safety of APD in treating delirium. Since randomized controlled trials (RCT) are costly and time-expensive, we aim to approach the research question of the efficacy of APD in the treatment of delirium using retrospective cohort analysis. We plan to use the Causal inference framework to look for the underlying causal structure model, leveraging the availability of large observational data on ICU patients. To explore safety outcomes associated with APD, we aim to build a causal model for delirium in the ICU using large observational data sets connecting various covariates correlated with delirium. We utilized the MIMIC III database, an extensive electronic health records (EHR) dataset with 53,423 distinct hospital admissions. Our null hypothesis is: there is no significant difference in outcomes for delirium patients under different drug-group in the ICU. Through our exploratory, machine learning based and causal analysis, we had findings such as: mean length-of-stay and max length-of-stay is higher for patients in Haloperidol drug group, and haloperidol group has a higher rate of death in a year compared to other two-groups. Our generated causal model explicitly shows the functional relationships between different covariates. For future work, we plan to do time-varying analysis on the dataset.

Delirium does not have any biomarker-based diagnosis and is commonly treated with antipsychotic drugs (APD). However, multiple studies have shown controversy over the efficacy or safety of APD in treating delirium. Since randomized controlled trials (RCT) are costly and time-expensive, we aim to approach the research question of the efficacy of APD in the treatment of delirium using retrospective cohort analysis. We plan to use the Causal inference framework to look for the underlying causal structure model, leveraging the availability of large observational data on ICU patients. To explore safety outcomes associated with APD, we aim to build a causal model for delirium in the ICU using large observational data sets connecting various covariates correlated with delirium. We utilized the MIMIC III database, an extensive electronic health records (EHR) dataset with 53,423 distinct hospital admissions. Our null hypothesis is: there is no significant difference in outcomes for delirium patients under different drug-group in the ICU. Through our exploratory, machine learning based and causal analysis, we had findings such as: mean length-of-stay and max length-of-stay is higher for patients in Haloperidol drug group, and haloperidol group has a higher rate of death in a year compared to other two-groups. Our generated causal model explicitly shows the functional relationships between different covariates. For future work, we plan to do time-varying analysis on the dataset.
abstract_zh: 重症监护病房 (ICU) 中约 80% 的病例发生谵妄，并与住院时间延长、死亡率增加和其他相关问题相关。谵妄症没有任何基于生物标志物的诊断，通常用抗精神病药物 (APD) 治疗。然而，多项研究表明 APD 治疗谵妄的有效性或安全性存在争议。由于随机对照试验 (RCT) 成本高昂且耗时，我们旨在通过回顾性队列分析来解决 APD 治疗谵妄疗效的研究问题。我们计划利用 ICU 患者的大量观察数据的可用性，使用因果推理框架来寻找潜在的因果结构模型。为了探索与 APD 相关的安全结果，我们的目标是使用连接与谵妄相关的各种协变量的大型观察数据集，建立 ICU 中谵妄的因果模型。我们利用 MIMIC III 数据库，这是一个广泛的电子健康记录 (EHR) 数据集，包含 53,423 条不同的入院记录。我们的零假设是：ICU 中不同药物组的谵妄患者的结局没有显着差异。通过我们基于机器学习的探索性和因果分析，我们发现：与其他两组相比，氟哌啶醇药物组患者的平均住院时间和最大住院时间较高，并且氟哌啶醇组的一年死亡率较高。我们生成的因果模型明确显示了不同协变量之间的函数关系。对于未来的工作，我们计划对数据集进行时变分析。

AI.tldr: 该论文利用大型电子健康记录（EHR）数据集，通过因果推断框架研究抗精神病药物对ICU谵妄患者的影响。
AI.motivation: 谵妄在ICU患者中高发且缺乏生物标志物诊断，抗精神病药物（APD）的疗效和安全性存在争议，而随机对照试验成本高昂，因此需通过观察性数据探索因果关系。
AI.method: 基于MIMIC III数据库（含53,423例住院记录），采用因果推断和机器学习方法构建谵妄的因果模型，分析APD治疗效果及安全性。
AI.result: 发现Haloperidol组患者平均住院时间更长，一年内死亡率较高；因果模型揭示了不同协变量间的功能关系。
AI.conclusion: Haloperidol可能与较差预后相关，因果模型有助于从观察性数据中挖掘潜在关联，未来需进一步开展时变分析。

For future work, we plan to do time-varying analysis on the dataset. 重症监护病房 (ICU) 中约 80% 的病例发生谵妄，并与住院时间延长、死亡率增加和其他相关问题相关。谵妄症没有任何基于生物标志物的诊断，通常用抗精神病药物 (APD) 治疗。然而，多项研究表明 APD 治疗谵妄的有效性或安全性存在争议。由于随机对照试验 (RCT) 成本高昂且耗时，我们旨在通过回顾性队列分析来解决 APD 治疗谵妄疗效的研究问题。我们计划利用 ICU 患者的大量观察数据的可用性，使用因果推理框架来寻找潜在的因果结构模型。为了探索与 APD 相关的安全结果，我们的目标是使用连接与谵妄相关的各种协变量的大型观察数据集，建立 ICU 中谵妄的因果模型。我们利用 MIMIC III 数据库，这是一个广泛的电子健康记录 (EHR) 数据集，包含 53,423 条不同的入院记录。我们的零假设是：ICU 中不同药物组的谵妄患者的结局没有显着差异。通过我们基于机器学习的探索性和因果分析，我们发现：与其他两组相比，氟哌啶醇药物组患者的平均住院时间和最大住院时间较高，并且氟哌啶醇组的一年死亡率较高。我们生成的因果模型明确显示了不同协变量之间的函数关系。对于未来的工作，我们计划对数据集进行时变分析。 该论文利用大型电子健康记录（EHR）数据集，通过因果推断框架研究抗精神病药物对ICU谵妄患者的影响。 Riddhiman Adib,Md Osman Gani,Sheikh Iqbal Ahamed,Mohammad Adibuzzaman cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Alessandro Ronca,Gabriel Paludo Licks,Giuseppe De Giacomo
title: Markov Abstractions for PAC Reinforcement Learning in Non-Markov Decision Processes
title_zh: 非马尔可夫决策过程中 PAC 强化学习的马尔可夫抽象
abstract: Our work aims at developing reinforcement learning algorithms that do not rely on the Markov assumption. We consider the class of Non-Markov Decision Processes where histories can be abstracted into a finite set of states while preserving the dynamics. We call it a Markov abstraction since it induces a Markov Decision Process over a set of states that encode the non-Markov dynamics. This phenomenon underlies the recently introduced Regular Decision Processes (as well as POMDPs where only a finite number of belief states is reachable). In all such kinds of decision process, an agent that uses a Markov abstraction can rely on the Markov property to achieve optimal behaviour. We show that Markov abstractions can be learned during reinforcement learning. Our approach combines automata learning and classic reinforcement learning. For these two tasks, standard algorithms can be employed. We show that our approach has PAC guarantees when the employed algorithms have PAC guarantees, and we also provide an experimental evaluation.
summary: Our work aims at developing reinforcement learning algorithms that do not rely on the Markov assumption. We consider the class of Non-Markov Decision Processes where histories can be abstracted into a finite set of states while preserving the dynamics. We call it a Markov abstraction since it induces a Markov Decision Process over a set of states that encode the non-Markov dynamics. This phenomenon underlies the recently introduced Regular Decision Processes (as well as POMDPs where only a finite number of belief states is reachable). In all such kinds of decision process, an agent that uses a Markov abstraction can rely on the Markov property to achieve optimal behaviour. We show that Markov abstractions can be learned during reinforcement learning. Our approach combines automata learning and classic reinforcement learning. For these two tasks, standard algorithms can be employed. We show that our approach has PAC guarantees when the employed algorithms have PAC guarantees, and we also provide an experimental evaluation.
abstract_zh: 我们的工作旨在开发不依赖马尔可夫假设的强化学习算法。我们考虑一类非马尔可夫决策过程，其中历史可以抽象为一组有限的状态，同时保留动态。我们称其为马尔可夫抽象，因为它在一组编码非马尔可夫动态的状态上引入马尔可夫决策过程。这种现象是最近引入的常规决策过程（以及只能达到有限数量的信念状态的 POMDP）的基础。在所有此类决策过程中，使用马尔可夫抽象的代理可以依靠马尔可夫属性来实现最佳行为。我们证明马尔可夫抽象可以在强化学习过程中学习。我们的方法结合了自动机学习和经典的强化学习。对于这两项任务，可以采用标准算法。我们证明，当所采用的算法具有 PAC 保证时，我们的方法具有 PAC 保证，并且我们还提供了实验评估。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在开发无需马尔可夫假设的强化学习算法，通过构建马尔可夫抽象来处理非马尔可夫决策过程（NMDPs）。
AI.motivation: 传统强化学习依赖马尔可夫假设，但在实际场景中环境可能具有非马尔可夫特性，需寻找无需该假设的解决方案。
AI.method: 结合自动机学习与经典强化学习方法，通过学习历史状态的有限抽象集构建马尔可夫抽象，并利用PAC算法保证学习效果。
AI.result: 提出的方法在保证PAC性质的前提下，通过实验验证了其在非马尔可夫环境中的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 通过学习马尔可夫抽象，代理可在非马尔可夫决策过程中实现最优行为，为复杂环境下的强化学习提供新思路。

authors_text: Shang Liu,Xiaobao Wei,Lulu Wang,Jing Zhang,Boyu Li,Haosong Yue
title: Center-of-Mass-based Robust Grasp Pose Adaptation Using RGBD Camera and Force/Torque Sensing
title_zh: 使用 RGBD 相机和力/扭矩传感的基于质心的鲁棒抓取姿势自适应
abstract: Object dropping may occur when the robotic arm grasps objects with uneven mass distribution due to additional moments generated by objects' gravity. To solve this problem, we present a novel work that does not require extra wrist and tactile sensors and large amounts of experiments for learning. First, we obtain the center-of-mass position of the rod object using the widely fixed joint torque sensors on the robot arm and RGBD camera. Further, we give the strategy of grasping to improve grasp stability. Simulation experiments are performed in "Mujoco". Results demonstrate that our work is effective in enhancing grasping robustness.
summary: Object dropping may occur when the robotic arm grasps objects with uneven mass distribution due to additional moments generated by objects' gravity. To solve this problem, we present a novel work that does not require extra wrist and tactile sensors and large amounts of experiments for learning. First, we obtain the center-of-mass position of the rod object using the widely fixed joint torque sensors on the robot arm and RGBD camera. Further, we give the strategy of grasping to improve grasp stability. Simulation experiments are performed in "Mujoco". Results demonstrate that our work is effective in enhancing grasping robustness.
abstract_zh: 当机械臂抓取质量分布不均匀的物体时，由于物体重力产生的额外力矩，可能会发生物体掉落。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一项新颖的工作，不需要额外的手腕和触觉传感器以及大量的实验来学习。首先，我们使用机械臂上广泛固定的关节扭矩传感器和 RGBD 相机来获取杆状物体的质心位置。此外，我们给出了抓取策略以提高抓取稳定性。模拟实验在“Mujoco”中进行。结果表明，我们的工作在增强抓取鲁棒性方面是有效的。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于质心的鲁棒抓取姿态自适应方法，通过RGBD相机和力/扭矩传感器提升机械臂抓取质量分布不均物体的稳定性。
AI.motivation: 传统方法在抓取质量分布不均物体时易因重力力矩导致物体掉落，且依赖额外传感器或大量实验数据。
AI.method: 利用机械臂关节扭矩传感器与RGBD相机估计物体质心位置，结合抓取策略优化姿态以提升稳定性。
AI.result: Mujoco仿真结果验证了该方法在增强抓取鲁棒性方面的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 无需额外传感器和大量实验，本文方法可有效提升机械臂抓取质量分布不均物体的稳定性，具有实际应用价值。

authors_text: Abdulla All noman,Umma Habiba Akter,Tahmid Hasan Pranto,AKM Bahalul Haque
title: Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence in Circular Economy: A Bibliometric Analysis and Systematic Literature Review
title_zh: 循环经济中的机器学习和人工智能：文献计量分析和系统文献综述
abstract: With unorganized, unplanned and improper use of limited raw materials, an abundant amount of waste is being produced, which is harmful to our environment and ecosystem. While traditional linear production lines fail to address far-reaching issues like waste production and a shorter product life cycle, a prospective concept, namely circular economy (CE), has shown promising prospects to be adopted at industrial and governmental levels. CE aims to complete the product life cycle loop by bringing out the highest values from raw materials in the design phase and later on by reusing, recycling, and remanufacturing. Innovative technologies like artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning(ML) provide vital assistance in effectively adopting and implementing CE in real-world practices. This study explores the adoption and integration of applied AI techniques in CE. First, we conducted bibliometric analysis on a collection of 104 SCOPUS indexed documents exploring the critical research criteria in AI and CE. Forty papers were picked to conduct a systematic literature review from these documents. The selected documents were further divided into six categories: sustainable development, reverse logistics, waste management, supply chain management, recycle & reuse, and manufacturing development. Comprehensive research insights and trends have been extracted and delineated. Finally, the research gap needing further attention has been identified and the future research directions have also been discussed.
summary: With unorganized, unplanned and improper use of limited raw materials, an abundant amount of waste is being produced, which is harmful to our environment and ecosystem. While traditional linear production lines fail to address far-reaching issues like waste production and a shorter product life cycle, a prospective concept, namely circular economy (CE), has shown promising prospects to be adopted at industrial and governmental levels. CE aims to complete the product life cycle loop by bringing out the highest values from raw materials in the design phase and later on by reusing, recycling, and remanufacturing. Innovative technologies like artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning(ML) provide vital assistance in effectively adopting and implementing CE in real-world practices. This study explores the adoption and integration of applied AI techniques in CE. First, we conducted bibliometric analysis on a collection of 104 SCOPUS indexed documents exploring the critical research criteria in AI and CE. Forty papers were picked to conduct a systematic literature review from these documents. The selected documents were further divided into six categories: sustainable development, reverse logistics, waste management, supply chain management, recycle & reuse, and manufacturing development. Comprehensive research insights and trends have been extracted and delineated. Finally, the research gap needing further attention has been identified and the future research directions have also been discussed.
abstract_zh: 由于无组织、无计划和不当使用有限的原材料，产生了大量的废物，这对我们的环境和生态系统有害。虽然传统的线性生产线无法解决废物产生和产品生命周期缩短等影响深远的问题，但循环经济（CE）这一前瞻性概念已显示出在工业和政府层面采用的良好前景。 CE 旨在通过在设计阶段以及随后的再利用、回收和再制造中发挥原材料的最高价值来完成产品生命周期循环。人工智能 (AI) 和机器学习 (ML) 等创新技术为在现实世界实践中有效采用和实施 CE 提供了重要帮助。本研究探讨了应用人工智能技术在 CE 中的采用和集成。首先，我们对 104 个 SCOPUS 索引文档集进行了文献计量分析，探索人工智能和 CE 的关键研究标准。我们挑选了 40 篇论文来对这些文件进行系统的文献综述。所选文件进一步分为六类：可持续发展、逆向物流、废物管理、供应链管理、回收和再利用以及制造发展。提取并描绘了全面的研究见解和趋势。最后，明确了需要进一步关注的研究空白，并讨论了未来的研究方向。

AI.tldr: 本文通过文献计量分析和系统综述探讨人工智能与机器学习在循环经济中的应用现状与趋势。
AI.motivation: 传统线性生产模式无法有效解决资源浪费和产品生命周期短的问题，而循环经济需借助AI/ML技术实现资源高效利用。
AI.method: 对104篇SCOPUS收录文献进行文献计量分析，选取40篇论文按六大主题分类开展系统综述。
AI.result: 提取了可持续发展、逆向物流等领域的研究趋势，识别出技术整合度不足、跨学科研究欠缺等研究空白。
AI.conclusion: AI/ML技术对循环经济实施具有重要价值，未来需加强技术实际应用验证及多学科协同研究。

search_text: Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence in Circular Economy: A Bibliometric Analysis and Systematic Literature Review 循环经济中的机器学习和人工智能：文献计量分析和系统文献综述 With unorganized, unplanned and improper use of limited raw materials, an abundant amount of waste is being produced, which is harmful to our environment and ecosystem. While traditional linear production lines fail to address far-reaching issues like waste production and a shorter product life cycle, a prospective concept, namely circular economy (CE), has shown promising prospects to be adopted at industrial and governmental levels. CE aims to complete the product life cycle loop by bringing out the highest values from raw materials in the design phase and later on by reusing, recycling, and remanufacturing. Innovative technologies like artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning(ML) provide vital assistance in effectively adopting and implementing CE in real-world practices. This study explores the adoption and integration of applied AI techniques in CE. First, we conducted bibliometric analysis on a collection of 104 SCOPUS indexed documents exploring the critical research criteria in AI and CE. Forty papers were picked to conduct a systematic literature review from these documents. The selected documents were further divided into six categories: sustainable development, reverse logistics, waste management, supply chain management, recycle & reuse, and manufacturing development. Comprehensive research insights and trends have been extracted and delineated. Finally, the research gap needing further attention has been identified and the future research directions have also been discussed. 由于无组织、无计划和不当使用有限的原材料，产生了大量的废物，这对我们的环境和生态系统有害。虽然传统的线性生产线无法解决废物产生和产品生命周期缩短等影响深远的问题，但循环经济（CE）这一前瞻性概念已显示出在工业和政府层面采用的良好前景。 CE 旨在通过在设计阶段以及随后的再利用、回收和再制造中发挥原材料的最高价值来完成产品生命周期循环。人工智能 (AI) 和机器学习 (ML) 等创新技术为在现实世界实践中有效采用和实施 CE 提供了重要帮助。本研究探讨了应用人工智能技术在 CE 中的采用和集成。首先，我们对 104 个 SCOPUS 索引文档集进行了文献计量分析，探索人工智能和 CE 的关键研究标准。我们挑选了 40 篇论文来对这些文件进行系统的文献综述。所选文件进一步分为六类：可持续发展、逆向物流、废物管理、供应链管理、回收和再利用以及制造发展。提取并描绘了全面的研究见解和趋势。最后，明确了需要进一步关注的研究空白，并讨论了未来的研究方向。 本文通过文献计量分析和系统综述探讨人工智能与机器学习在循环经济中的应用现状与趋势。 Abdulla All noman,Umma Habiba Akter,Tahmid Hasan Pranto,AKM Bahalul Haque cs.CY,cs.AI

title: Demographic-Reliant Algorithmic Fairness: Characterizing the Risks of Demographic Data Collection in the Pursuit of Fairness
title_zh: 依赖人口统计的算法公平性：描述追求公平的人口统计数据收集的风险
comment: 21 pages, accepted to FAccT 2022. Updated to camera ready version: added a section defining how we use demographic data, clarified distinction between sections 5.2 and 6.2, additional line edits
comments: 21 pages, accepted to FAccT 2022. Updated to camera ready version: added a section defining how we use demographic data, clarified distinction between sections 5.2 and 6.2, additional line edits
abstract: Most proposed algorithmic fairness techniques require access to data on a "sensitive attribute" or "protected category" (such as race, ethnicity, gender, or sexuality) in order to make performance comparisons and standardizations across groups, however this data is largely unavailable in practice, hindering the widespread adoption of algorithmic fairness. Through this paper, we consider calls to collect more data on demographics to enable algorithmic fairness and challenge the notion that discrimination can be overcome with smart enough technical methods and sufficient data alone. We show how these techniques largely ignore broader questions of data governance and systemic oppression when categorizing individuals for the purpose of fairer algorithmic processing. In this work, we explore under what conditions demographic data should be collected and used to enable algorithmic fairness methods by characterizing a range of social risks to individuals and communities. For the risks to individuals we consider the unique privacy risks associated with the sharing of sensitive attributes likely to be the target of fairness analysis, the possible harms stemming from miscategorizing and misrepresenting individuals in the data collection process, and the use of sensitive data beyond data subjects' expectations. Looking more broadly, the risks to entire groups and communities include the expansion of surveillance infrastructure in the name of fairness, misrepresenting and mischaracterizing what it means to be part of a demographic group or to hold a certain identity, and ceding the ability to define for themselves what constitutes biased or unfair treatment. We argue that, by confronting these questions before and during the collection of demographic data, algorithmic fairness methods are more likely to actually mitigate harmful treatment disparities without reinforcing systems of oppression.
summary: Most proposed algorithmic fairness techniques require access to data on a "sensitive attribute" or "protected category" (such as race, ethnicity, gender, or sexuality) in order to make performance comparisons and standardizations across groups, however this data is largely unavailable in practice, hindering the widespread adoption of algorithmic fairness. Through this paper, we consider calls to collect more data on demographics to enable algorithmic fairness and challenge the notion that discrimination can be overcome with smart enough technical methods and sufficient data alone. We show how these techniques largely ignore broader questions of data governance and systemic oppression when categorizing individuals for the purpose of fairer algorithmic processing. In this work, we explore under what conditions demographic data should be collected and used to enable algorithmic fairness methods by characterizing a range of social risks to individuals and communities. For the risks to individuals we consider the unique privacy risks associated with the sharing of sensitive attributes likely to be the target of fairness analysis, the possible harms stemming from miscategorizing and misrepresenting individuals in the data collection process, and the use of sensitive data beyond data subjects' expectations. Looking more broadly, the risks to entire groups and communities include the expansion of surveillance infrastructure in the name of fairness, misrepresenting and mischaracterizing what it means to be part of a demographic group or to hold a certain identity, and ceding the ability to define for themselves what constitutes biased or unfair treatment. We argue that, by confronting these questions before and during the collection of demographic data, algorithmic fairness methods are more likely to actually mitigate harmful treatment disparities without reinforcing systems of oppression.
abstract_zh: 大多数提出的算法公平技术需要访问“敏感属性”或“受保护类别”（例如种族、民族、性别或性取向）的数据，以便进行跨群体的性能比较和标准化，但这些数据在实践中基本上不可用，阻碍了算法公平的广泛采用。通过本文，我们考虑呼吁收集更多人口统计数据，以实现算法公平性，并挑战仅靠足够聪明的技术方法和足够的数据就可以克服歧视的观念。我们展示了这些技术在为了更公平的算法处理而对个人进行分类时如何在很大程度上忽略了更广泛的数据治理和系统性压迫问题。在这项工作中，我们探讨了在什么条件下应该收集和使用人口数据，通过描述个人和社区的一系列社会风险来实现算法公平方法。对于个人面临的风险，我们认为与共享敏感属性相关的独特隐私风险可能是公平性分析的目标，在数据收集过程中错误分类和歪曲个人可能造成的危害，以及超出数据主体预期的敏感数据的使用。从更广泛的角度来看，整个群体和社区面临的风险包括以公平为名扩大监视基础设施、歪曲和错误描述作为某个人口群体的一部分或持有某种身份的含义，以及放弃自己定义什么构成偏见或不公平待遇的能力。我们认为，通过在收集人口数据之前和期间面对这些问题，算法公平方法更有可能在不强化压迫制度的情况下真正减轻有害的待遇差异。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨在算法公平性研究中收集人口统计数据的风险，挑战单纯依赖数据和技术手段消除歧视的观点，并分析数据收集过程中可能加剧系统性压迫的问题。
AI.motivation: 当前算法公平性技术依赖敏感属性数据，但实际中此类数据难以获取且存在治理风险，需重新审视数据收集的伦理和社会影响。
AI.method: 通过分析个体隐私风险、误分类危害及群体层面的监控扩张等社会风险，结合数据治理与系统性压迫的理论框架进行批判性研究。
AI.result: 识别出数据收集可能带来的隐私泄露、身份误标、监控强化及群体自我定义权丧失等风险，揭示技术手段无法独立解决结构性不公。
AI.conclusion: 算法公平性实践需在数据收集阶段前置伦理考量，避免技术方案加剧而非缓解社会压迫，强调公平性与社会正义的协同推进。

authors_text: David Leslie,Michael Katell,Mhairi Aitken,Jatinder Singh,Morgan Briggs,Rosamund Powell,Cami Rincón,Antonella Perini,Smera Jayadeva,Christopher Burr
title: Data Justice in Practice: A Guide for Developers
title_zh: 实践中的数据正义：开发人员指南
comment: arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2202.02776
comments: arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2202.02776
abstract: The Advancing Data Justice Research and Practice project aims to broaden understanding of the social, historical, cultural, political, and economic forces that contribute to discrimination and inequity in contemporary ecologies of data collection, governance, and use. This is the consultation draft of a guide for developers and organisations, which are producing, procuring, or using data-intensive technologies.In the first section, we introduce the field of data justice, from its early discussions to more recent proposals to relocate understandings of what data justice means. This section includes a description of the six pillars of data justice around which this guidance revolves. Next, to support developers in designing, developing, and deploying responsible and equitable data-intensive and AI/ML systems, we outline the AI/ML project lifecycle through a sociotechnical lens. To support the operationalisation data justice throughout the entirety of the AI/ML lifecycle and within data innovation ecosystems, we then present five overarching principles of responsible, equitable, and trustworthy data research and innovation practices, the SAFE-D principles-Safety, Accountability, Fairness, Explainability, and Data Quality, Integrity, Protection, and Privacy. The final section presents guiding questions that will help developers both address data justice issues throughout the AI/ML lifecycle and engage in reflective innovation practices that ensure the design, development, and deployment of responsible and equitable data-intensive and AI/ML systems.
summary: The Advancing Data Justice Research and Practice project aims to broaden understanding of the social, historical, cultural, political, and economic forces that contribute to discrimination and inequity in contemporary ecologies of data collection, governance, and use. This is the consultation draft of a guide for developers and organisations, which are producing, procuring, or using data-intensive technologies.In the first section, we introduce the field of data justice, from its early discussions to more recent proposals to relocate understandings of what data justice means. This section includes a description of the six pillars of data justice around which this guidance revolves. Next, to support developers in designing, developing, and deploying responsible and equitable data-intensive and AI/ML systems, we outline the AI/ML project lifecycle through a sociotechnical lens. To support the operationalisation data justice throughout the entirety of the AI/ML lifecycle and within data innovation ecosystems, we then present five overarching principles of responsible, equitable, and trustworthy data research and innovation practices, the SAFE-D principles-Safety, Accountability, Fairness, Explainability, and Data Quality, Integrity, Protection, and Privacy. The final section presents guiding questions that will help developers both address data justice issues throughout the AI/ML lifecycle and engage in reflective innovation practices that ensure the design, development, and deployment of responsible and equitable data-intensive and AI/ML systems.
abstract_zh: 推进数据正义研究和实践项目旨在扩大对社会、历史、文化、政治和经济力量的理解，这些力量导致了当代数据收集、治理和使用生态中的歧视和不平等。这是为生产、采购或使用数据密集型技术的开发人员和组织提供的指南的咨询草案。在第一部分中，我们介绍了数据正义领域，从早期的讨论到最近提出的重新理解数据正义含义的建议。本节描述了本指南所围绕的数据正义的六大支柱。接下来，为了支持开发人员设计、开发和部署负责任且公平的数据密集型和 AI/ML 系统，我们从社会技术角度概述了 AI/ML 项目生命周期。为了支持整个 AI/ML 生命周期和数据创新生态系统内的可操作数据正义，我们提出了负责任、公平和值得信赖的数据研究和创新实践的五项总体原则，即 SAFE-D 原则——安全、问责、公平、可解释性以及数据质量、完整性、保护和隐私。最后一部分提出了指导性问题，将帮助开发人员解决整个 AI/ML 生命周期中的数据正义问题，并参与反思性创新实践，以确保设计、开发和部署负责任且公平的数据密集型和 AI/ML 系统。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在为开发者和组织提供实践指南，帮助其在数据密集型技术的设计与应用中落实数据正义原则。
AI.motivation: 应对数据技术应用中因社会、历史、文化等因素导致的歧视与不平等现象，推动负责任且公平的数据实践。
AI.method: 通过梳理数据正义的六个核心支柱，结合AI/ML项目生命周期的社技视角，提出SAFE-D原则（安全、问责、公平等）并设计指导问题。

AI.conclusion: 该指南为开发者提供了可操作的数据正义实施路径，有助于构建更公平、可信的数据技术生态。
